LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST, DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER REDDING'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING THAT THERE IS AN MDL EXCEPTION TO THE GENERAL RULE THAT A FEDERAL DISTRICT COURT DECISION IS NOT LAW OF THE CASE ON REMAND AND THAT THE JULY 21, 2011 ORDER OF JUDGE CARR APPLIES TO THIS CASE, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO CARRY ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PREVAIL ON SOME ELEMENT OF ITS CASE BECAUSE IT HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE HER SYMPTOMS OCCURRED WITHIN ONE HOUR AFTER SHE WAS GIVEN HEPARIN OR THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE SHE COULD COME WITHIN THE HIT EXCEPTION TO THE ONE HOUR RULE ANNOUNCED IN THE FEDERAL COURT ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
NOTICE OF SETTLEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST, DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER REDDING

Mtn Toi Exclude And/Or Strike Opinions And Evidence Of Drs. Kiss And Gershwin
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jonz Norine, Esq. of Kenny Snowden & Norine, Tel. 530.225.8990, for defendant and moving party;
Leslie M. Mitchell, Esq. of the Law Office of Leslie M. Mitchell, Tel. 916.849.1759, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST, DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER REDDING's Motion to Exclude And/Or Strike Opinions And Evidence Of Drs. Kiss And Gershwin. The motion to exclude is treated as an evidentiary objection, which the Court does not reach because the declarations are not relevant to the Court's reasoning. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on Defendant Catholic Healthcare West, d/b/a Mercy Medical Center's Motion to Continue Trial.
Dept: 220; Judge: James J. McBride; Clerk: Kevin R. Dougherty; CSR: Not Reported.

Leslie M. Mitchell, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Katherine Davis.
Jonz Mitchell, Esq. appeared for defendant Catholic Healthcare West, d/b/a Mercy Medical Center.

Case is called and appearances stated. The Court reviews the stipulation from counsel to continue trial. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is set to be heard in Department 302 at 9:30 this morning. The Tentative Ruling - Defendant's MSJ is DENIED. Department 220 agrees to issue a new trial date.

Good cause appearing, Trial in this matter is CONTINUED to Tuesday, October 15, 2013 @ 9:30 a.m. in Department 206.

Stipulation and Order to Continue Trial was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jonz Norine, Esq. of Kenny Snowden & Norine, Tel. 530.225.8990, for defendant and moving party;
Leslie M. Mitchell, Esq. of the Law Office of Leslie M. Mitchell, Tel. 916.849.1759, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST, DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER REDDING's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Even assuming that there is an MDL exception to the general rule that a federal district court decision is not law of the case on remand and that the July 21, 2011 Order of Judge Carr applies to this case, Defendant has failed to carry its initial burden of showing that Plaintiff cannot prevail on some element of its case because it has failed to present evidence showing that Plaintiff cannot prove her symptoms occurred within one hour after she was given heparin or that Plaintiff cannot prove she could come within the HIT exception to the one hour rule announced in the federal court order. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
